steps.title: Identify the defining contrast
steps.content: The passage contrasts single-species conservation (focus on one vulnerable species) with ecosystem-based conservation (consider interactions of climate, terrain, and multiple species). The ecosystem approach means strategies address the broader system, not M. guerreronis alone.

steps.title: Apply the logic to outcomes
steps.content: Ecosystem-based conservation addresses multiple system elements (climate, terrain, various species), not exclusively M. guerreronis, yet the passage says such approaches lead to “better outcomes for all the species” — so broader strategies still protect the target. B states this exactly: conservation strategies not exclusively aimed at M. guerreronis benefit the species nonetheless. The others misidentify the mechanism or contradict the passage. That matches choice B — B is correct.
